heroImageAlt: Two people chatting comfortably during a counselling support session
mainIntro1: Our counselling and emotional support sessions are available to any individual, carer or family member that has been affected by a palliative condition.
mainIntro2: Our qualified counsellors are on hand to listen to anyone who feels they might benefit from a safe, confidential space to talk. Conversations are welcome around any aspect of life (whether related to the diagnosis or not), helping individuals feel heard and making sure they do not have to face a difficult time alone.
mainIntro3: To find out more about the support we can offer, click on the links below:

cards.title: Counselling
cards.excerpt: Confidential one-to-one sessions with qualified counsellors for patients, family members, and unpaid carers.
cards.imageAlt: A quiet, private conversation in a safe environment

cards.title: Bereavement Support
cards.excerpt: A listening ear, groups, and signposting for anyone affected by loss or preparing for a bereavement.
cards.imageAlt: Countryside and sky after a storm, symbolising hope and reflection

cards.title: Support Groups
cards.excerpt: Carer support groups offering connection, peer support, and signposting when needed.
cards.imageAlt: People in a welcoming group conversation

cards.title: Resource Hub
cards.excerpt: Trusted organisations, leaflets, and practical links to help with bereavement, grief, and financial support.

referralIntro: To enquire about any of our services please complete the
referralOutro: and a member of our team will be in touch.
